creator: bofh@freebsd.org
text:
When running apache24 I cannot see any perl process at all.

# sockstat -l
USER     COMMAND    PID   FD PROTO  LOCAL ADDRESS         FOREIGN ADDRESS      
www      httpd      30954 3  tcp6   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30954 4  tcp4   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30953 3  tcp6   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30953 4  tcp4   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30952 3  tcp6   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30952 4  tcp4   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30951 3  tcp6   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30951 4  tcp4   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30950 3  tcp6   *:80                  *:*
www      httpd      30950 4  tcp4   *:80                  *:*
root     httpd      30944 3  tcp6   *:80                  *:*
root     httpd      30944 4  tcp4   *:80                  *:*
root     syslogd    30852 5  dgram  /var/run/log
root     syslogd    30852 6  dgram  /var/run/logpriv

# ps aux | grep perl
root     31357  0.0  0.0 12892  2448  2  S+J  01:02   0:00.00 grep perl

So this must be some of your other services that is running a perl process which I cannot debug. In our setup we have nearly 60+ servers and more than 200 jails running php(both 8.1 and 8.2) with nginx whcih we regularly update without any issues. And most of our servers have also been updated to 13.2 with pkg upgrade -fy without any issues.

Check service -e to debug what service running and isolate the perl process. Otherwise this is not a bug. And in most cases I upgrade the port in our local pkg builder, build and do upgrade test in some of our servers before even committing to the ports tree.

If you need more help with isolating the script check the mailing lists or in IRC if someone can help.
